section1.title: Why invest in a mobile app?
section1.p1: Mobile is where users spend the most time. A branded app creates a direct channel to your audience — no middlemen, no cost per click. Push notifications, offline support, and deep device integrations deliver an experience that a mobile website simply cannot match.
section1.p2: We build mobile apps for iOS and Android: from focused MVPs that validate an idea in weeks, to full-featured platforms with real-time data, payments, maps, and AI.

section2.title: When does a business need a mobile app?
section2.lead: A mobile app makes strategic sense when:
items: You need a direct channel to customers with push notifications and re-engagement
Your product relies on device features: camera, GPS, NFC, biometrics
Users return frequently and expect a fast, offline-capable experience
You're building a marketplace, booking platform, or on-demand service
You want to reduce support load through self-service features
Competitors already have an app and it's affecting your retention
section2.footer: If two or more points apply — a mobile app will give you a measurable competitive edge.

section3.title: What you get
items: Native iOS (Swift) and/or Android (Kotlin) apps, or cross-platform (Flutter / React Native)
Backend and REST/GraphQL API, or integration with your existing backend
App Store and Google Play publishing with full configuration
Push notifications, in-app messaging, and analytics
Offline mode and local data sync
Custom UX/UI design tailored to mobile patterns
QA testing on real devices (iOS + Android)

section4.title: How we work
section4.lead: We follow a structured, transparent process:
items: Discovery and scoping — we align on goals, user flows, and technical requirements. We help choose the right stack (native vs. cross-platform) based on your use case and budget.
Design — UX wireframes first, then high-fidelity screens. We design for real device sizes and platform guidelines (iOS Human Interface, Material Design).
Development — iterative sprints with working builds delivered every 1–2 weeks. You can test on your own device throughout the process.
QA and release — we test on real iOS and Android devices, handle App Store and Google Play submission, and manage review cycles.
section4.footer: Typical timeline: 8–20 weeks depending on scope. MVPs can launch faster.

section5.title: Support and evolution
section5.p1: After launch we handle OS compatibility updates, bug fixes, and new feature development. Mobile apps need ongoing maintenance — iOS and Android release major OS updates annually that require adaptation.
section5.p2: We can work on a retainer for continuous product development, or handle targeted updates as needed. Your app stays stable, compliant, and up to date.

mobileFaq.q: Should I build for iOS first or Android?
mobileFaq.a: It depends on your audience. In most cases, starting cross-platform (Flutter or React Native) covers both platforms from day one with a single codebase — which is the most cost-effective approach. If your audience is heavily concentrated on one platform, we can prioritize that one.

mobileFaq.q: What's the difference between native and cross-platform development?
mobileFaq.a: Native apps (Swift for iOS, Kotlin for Android) deliver maximum performance and full device API access but require two separate codebases. Cross-platform tools like Flutter and React Native share most of the code between platforms, reducing cost and time while still producing near-native quality. We recommend cross-platform for most business apps; native for high-performance or platform-specific products.

mobileFaq.q: How long does mobile app development take?
mobileFaq.a: A focused MVP typically takes 8–12 weeks. A full-featured app with custom backend, complex logic, and integrations takes 16–24 weeks. The timeline depends heavily on scope, design complexity, and how quickly decisions are made.

mobileFaq.q: Do you handle App Store and Google Play publishing?
mobileFaq.a: Yes. We set up developer accounts (or work with yours), prepare all required assets — screenshots, descriptions, icons — and manage the submission and review process. We have experience resolving App Store review rejections.

mobileFaq.q: Do you also build the backend?
mobileFaq.a: Yes, if needed. We build REST or GraphQL APIs, databases, and admin panels. If you already have a backend, we integrate with it via API. Full-stack delivery is our standard mode.

mobileFaq.q: Can the app work without an internet connection?
mobileFaq.a: Yes. We design offline-first architecture where needed: local storage, background sync, and graceful degradation when connectivity is poor. This is especially important for field service, delivery, and logistics apps.

mobileFaq.q: How are app updates delivered to users?
mobileFaq.a: Updates are published through App Store and Google Play. Critical fixes can be fast-tracked. For React Native and Flutter apps, some changes can be delivered via over-the-air (OTA) updates without going through store review.

mobileFaq.q: How much does a mobile app cost?
mobileFaq.a: Cost depends on the platform (iOS only, Android only, or both), app complexity, and required integrations. Use our calculator for a rough estimate, or contact us for a detailed breakdown.

custom.label: Native (Swift / Kotlin)
pros: Full access to all device APIs
Best compliance with platform UX patterns
Ideal for complex animations and hardware features
cons: Requires two teams or sequential development

offshelf.label: Cross-platform (Flutter / React Native)
pros: Single codebase for iOS and Android
Near-native quality in most use cases
cons: Some platform-specific APIs need extra bridging
